name: Spadewink
concept: A round, velvet-plum digger mole with mirror-polished shovel paws who carries a permanent little mound of packed earth on his back — a portable garden with one fat clover sprouting from it, the only green thing he has ever grown and he will not let anyone touch it.
imagePrompt:
Chunky pixel-art creature sprite, full body, standing upright, facing the viewer, symmetrical and centered. Hard-edged square pixels, no anti-aliasing, no blur, no gradients — a tight limited palette of about 10 flat colours with 2-step blocky dithered shading and a solid 1-pixel dark-plum outline all the way around a single clean unbroken silhouette.

SILHOUETTE: one bulbous egg-shaped body, wider at the bottom, like a heavy little bean that sits firmly on the ground — plus one rounded bump rising off the top of the back. The outline reads instantly at thumbnail size: fat rounded dome, two enormous paws flanking the body, one hill on the back. No gaps, no floating pieces, nothing thinner than a chunky 4-pixel block.

PROPORTIONS: head and body are one continuous mass, roughly 60% of the total height. The two front paws are comically oversized — each nearly as wide as the head — held low and forward, resting flat on the ground like a pair of spades, so the creature is propped on them. Tiny stubby hind feet peek out below as two rounded nubs. A short thick carrot-shaped tail-stub at the base, fully attached, no taper to a point.

PALETTE: the bold colour is a deep velvet plum-violet fur body (rich, slightly dusty, unmistakably ONE hero colour), with a warm cream-buff oval belly patch and cream muzzle. The shovel paws are a completely different material — smooth brassy-bronze, polished to a mirror sheen, rendered with a hard flat highlight block on each paw like a well-used spoon. The mound of earth on his back is warm terracotta and cocoa brown, drawn as chunky stacked pixel clods with visible crumbly blocky texture. Growing directly out of the crown of that mound is a single fat clover: a thick 4-pixel-wide cocoa stem and three big rounded mint-green leaves, solid and chubby, no wisps, no stray shoots, welded to the mound.

FACE: extremely simple and honest. Two tiny eyes squeezed shut into upward happy crescent arcs — thick 2-pixel dark strokes that make him look permanently, contentedly squinting, because he has lived his whole life in the dark. Between and below them, a big soft heart-shaped rose-pink nose taking up a generous chunk of the face, with a single bright highlight pixel on it. A tiny warm-blush pixel patch on each cheek. Two small round velvet ears sitting flush against the head, not sticking out.

MATERIAL: dense short velvet fur suggested by tight blocky dithering along the body edges; the earth mound is granular and matte; the paws are the only shiny thing on him. The contrast between soft matte plum fur and hard shiny metal-bright paws is the whole visual joke.

THE ONE IDEA: he is a tiny creature who dug so deep he brought a hill home with him, and the single clover on his back is his entire life's harvest — he stands proudly with his enormous polished paws planted, quietly guarding it.

Cute, warm, dignified, slightly silly. Round, weighty, huggable. Clear readable shapes.
